svg.text: gguf-chisel — patch GGUF metadata without moving a single tensor byte

g.text: patched model.gguf in place: 704 head bytes written, tensor data untouched
$ gguf-chisel template set model.gguf --preset llama3 # longer template
gguf-chisel: metadata head grew beyond the available space (need 752 bytes,
have 704); re-run with --rewrite (optionally -o NEW.gguf), and consider
--reserve to leave headroom for future in-place edits
rewrote model.gguf: 4878 head bytes (+4096 reserved), copied 160 bytes of tensor data
$ gguf-chisel set model.gguf "general.name=Sample 32k" # headroom absorbs it
set general.name: string "gguf-chisel sample model" -> string "Sample 32k"
patched model.gguf in place: 4896 head bytes written, tensor data untouched
$ gguf-chisel template check model.gguf && gguf-chisel verify model.gguf
gguf v3, 10 metadata keys, 2 tensors, data section 160 bytes
# one static binary, zero deps — the same edit on a 40 GB quant writes only the head
